sections.paragraph:
ἀναίσθητος, ον (Thu. et al., Philo; Jos., Ant. 11, 41) without feeling/perception (Hippocr., VM 15; Thrasymachus [IV b.c.]: 85 Fgm. B 1; Pla., Tim. 75e; Philostrat., Imag. 1, 23 p. 326, 20; Herm. Wr. 9, 9 ὁ θεὸς οὐκ ἀ.; Diogenes p. 116, 21 Malherbe) of cultic images of deities Dg 2:4; 3:3 (Ar. 13, 1).—DELG s.v. 1 ἀίω.

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Thrasymachus Thrasymachus , rhet./soph., V b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Diogenes Diogenes = letters attributed to the philosopher D. of Sinope; date uncertain—List 5

Malherbe Malherbe = AM., various works

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 64
